claim_boundary: The ledger proves only current registered behavior/source/owner/path accounting; sibling evidence and final release authority remain separate.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_bindings.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report

native_check_inventory.summary: Run the behavior commitment ledger model and failure variants.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Define the work-package boundary and select one of the six ledger modes.
Inventory fresh source surfaces and external commitments bidirectionally with one primary owner each.
Record dependencies, replacement/model-sync state, evidence, and internal PPA bindings for path-sensitive rows.
Run ledger and Primary Path Authority checks plus required DCAR/shard/risk projections.
Block broad confidence on any missing, extra, overlap, stale, dependency, disposition, or PPA gap.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Bind external behavior commitments to fresh sources, exactly one primary owner model, dependencies, evidence, and path authority.

do_not_use_when: The scope is helper internals, no external behavior inventory exists, or a sibling evidence route owns the question.
routes.summary: Bind external behavior commitments to fresh sources, exactly one primary owner model, dependencies, evidence, and path authority.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-behavior-commitment-ledger skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Every current source maps to commitments and every commitment maps back to fresh source evidence.
Missing DCAR, TestMesh, alignment, risk, replacement, dependency, or PPA evidence blocks broad confidence.
Each commitment has exactly one primary owner and path-sensitive rows consume internal Primary Path Authority evidence.
Accept a bounded source/behavior inventory and selected mode; register commitments and one owner each; block missing/extra/overlapping/stale rows or blocked PPA; hand canonical cases, shard.
Route id: behavior_commitment_ledger; role: public_owner; native owner: behavior_commitment_ledger. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill internally owns the primary_path_authority hand.
Use for bootstrap_ledger, add_behavior, change_behavior, remove_or_replace_behavior, coverage_gap_backfill, or model_miss_check.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Missing source coverage, owner overlap, unknown dependency, stale evidence, blocked PPA, or absent broad-claim shards/gates blocks broad confidence.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus commitment/source/owner/PPA status.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use for external behavior registration, bidirectional source coverage, exactly one primary owner model, change-mode account.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard validates ledger ownership but cannot manufacture commitments, PPA evidence, or release confidence.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not inventory helper internals or replace PPA, Model-Test Alignment, TestMesh, or Risk Evidence Ledger; return no-inventory ordinary modeling to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Bind path-sensitive rows through Primary Path Authority, run review_behavior_commitment_ledger(), and project DCAR/TestMesh/risk evidence.
Default replacement leaves no second successful path; scoped rows still need owner, reason, validation boundary, and disposition.
Define the boundary, choose the mode, and inventory fresh source surfaces bidirectionally.
Maintain the external behavior account book that binds promised behavior to current sources, one primary model owner, dependencies, evidence, and path authority.
Register external BehaviorCommitmentLedger rows with exactly one primary owner, dependencies, replacement/model-sync state, and evidence.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
